title: One injured in South End shooting
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: One person was injured in a shooting on Washington Street and East Berkeley Street, South End, Sunday evening.
The victim was taken to a local hospital with non-life-threatening injuries following the 7:42 p.m. incident.
Boston Police have not made any arrests yet and are actively investigating the shooting.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony is emerging as a superstar at World Baseball Classic
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony hit a game-winning home run for Team USA in a 2-1 win over the Dominican Republic.
The 21-year-old outfielder is batting .318 with two homers and seven RBI in the World Baseball Classic.
Anthony's performance spotlighted him as a rising talent, comparable to legends like DiMaggio and Jeter.

title: 5 Boston sports moments that just missed out on the Soundbites Bracket
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Rob Gronkowski's 2012 humorous Spanish mistake 'Yo soy fiesta' missed Boston.com's Soundbites Bracket.
The 'Mona Lisa Vito' reference from Bill Belichick during Deflategate was excluded for its lesser notoriety.
Dick Williams's 1967 prediction for Red Sox success was notable but didn't make the Soundbites Bracket cut.

title: Oscars 2026: The best jokes from Conan O’Brien’s monologue
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Conan O'Brien hosted the 2026 Oscars for the second year, delivering jokes on Netflix, Timothee Chalamet, and himself.
O'Brien's monologue featured skits and visual segments, including a joke about the Oscars airing only on YouTube in 2029.
Jimmy Kimmel joined O'Brien to present awards, making politically charged jokes, including a dig at CBS and North Korea.

title: Fundraiser: Community remembers Danvers mother, nurse killed in her home
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Janet Swallow, a 68-year-old nurse, was killed in her Danvers home by Anthony DeMayo, an 18-year-old high school student.
A GoFundMe for Janet Swallow's family has raised nearly $50,000, aiming for a $65,000 goal to help with their loss.
Anthony DeMayo faces murder charges and underwent a psychiatric evaluation after allegedly admitting to the attack.

title: Northeastern women’s hockey advances to Frozen Four after defeating Minnesota
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Northeastern women's hockey team advanced to the Frozen Four, winning 4-2 against Minnesota at Ridder Arena.
Sophomore goaltender Lisa Jönsson made 45 saves, while four Huskies players scored in the NCAA Quarterfinals victory.
The Huskies face No. 1 Ohio State on March 20 at Penn State's Pegula Ice Arena in the Frozen Four.

title: Bomb threat reportedly halts plane at Logan ‘for hours’ before being deemed ‘not credible’
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A JetBlue flight at Logan Airport was delayed due to a bomb threat deemed not credible by State Police.
The plane, bound for Vero Beach, Florida, remained on the tarmac for hours while authorities conducted a search.
An investigation has been opened, but no arrests have been made in connection with the bomb threat hoax.

title: 17 arrested during St. Patrick’s Day parade in South Boston, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: 17 individuals were arrested during the St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston, mainly due to public drinking.
Boston police could not provide specific charges or police reports regarding the arrests made at the parade.
The parade route changed this year, starting from Andrew Square, with increased policing and MBTA shuttle adjustments.

title: 2026 Oscars: Full list of winners (updating live)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "One Battle After Another" won Best Picture and five other Oscars, including Best Director and Best Supporting Actor.
"Sinners" won four Oscars: Best Original Screenplay, Best Cinematography, Best Score, and Best Actor for Michael B. Jordan.
"KPop Demon Hunters" secured the Best Animated Feature Oscar, and "Frankenstein" won for Best Production Design and Best Makeup.

title: Oscars red carpet: See the best Academy Awards fashion
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Hollywood stars, including Conan O'Brien, walked the red carpet before the 2026 Oscars at Dolby Theatre, Los Angeles.
Nominees Kate Hudson, Timothee Chalamet, and Elle Fanning showcased their fashion at the 98th Academy Awards.
Celebrities like Steven Spielberg, Leonardo DiCaprio, and Pedro Pascal were also present at the Oscars event.
